/p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; =
margin: 0 0 20px 0;">While the vast majority of people in tech will still v=
ote Democrat in November, this is a stunning turn for an industry that has =
long leaned hard to the left. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); =
font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">There have been a =
number of theories put forth to explain the right turn, most of which come =
in some flavor of: Trump better serves the interests of the self-interested=
 tech industry. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I think that story is almo=
st precisely </span><strong>backwards</strong><span>, and that until the De=
mocrats understand why, they’ll have a hard time winning back tech no=
 matter how loudly they yell “January 6th,” “felon,&#8221=
; or “fascist.” </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, =
55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">But I think t=
here’s a chance for the Democrats and tech to work together again. Pr=
esident Biden’s decision to drop out of the 2024 Presidential electio=
n is an opportunity for the Democratic party to focus on what can be, unbur=
dened by what has been. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-s=
ize: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">So while no one wants to=
 hear another VC’s political takes, I wanted to lay out as clearly as=
 I can the fundamental misunderstanding between the Democrats and tech as I=
 see it. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line=
-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">My hope is that the Democrats can face =
up to how they lost tech and use the chance they’ve been given to put=
 forth a more optimistic and progress-oriented platform that partners with =
tech to achieve the goals that both sides share. </p><p style=3D"color=
: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"=
>Now obviously, I don’t speak on behalf of the entire tech industry. =
This essay is an attempt to distill what I’ve been hearing and readin=
g over the last few months, and if you’d like, you can read it as my =
own personal views. When I write “People in tech,” you can subs=
titute “I.” </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-s=
ize: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>After a stint in Y=
oung Republicans in high school, I voted for Obama, then Hillary, then Bide=
n. I remember walking over the Brooklyn Bridge on an eerily overcast mornin=
g the day after Trump won in 2016 and thinking that we were entering the en=
d times. In 2020, I publicly supported Biden, </span><a href=3D"https://sub=
stack.com/redirect/054da7bb-5dbe-4e4f-aa4e-f6fb64bf758e?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3Q=
ifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"color: rgb(54=
, 55, 55); text-decoration: underline;">tweeting</a><span>: “We need =
a good person in the White House. Vote.” </span></p><p style=3D"=
color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20p=
x 0;">This time around, I don’t know who to vote for. Before Biden dr=
opped out, I found myself drifting towards Donald Trump even though I didn'=
t want to vote for him. Now, I’m waiting to see who the Democrats put=
 forth and the platform that new candidate proposes. </p><p style=3D"c=
olor: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px=
 0;"><strong>The Democrats lost tech.</strong><span> If they were running a=
gainst practically anyone other than Donald Trump, there might not be a sin=
gle blue vote in a Silicon Valley that’s historically bled blue.</spa=
n></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26p=
x; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><strong>They can win it back</strong><span>, and it=
 won’t be that hard. Let’s be serious: Donald Trump is not an i=
deal candidate for tech, he’s just the one that’s not hostile t=
o tech, for now. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-s=
ize: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">People have been faced w=
ith a “lesser of two evils” choice. They’ve lost faith in=
 both sides. Given that, many have chosen the side that at least says it wi=
ll get out of their way and let them build what the government can’t.=

-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Democrats lump tech pe=

ont-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">The Democrats look =

nt-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>As a country, =

</strong><span> component.</span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); fo=
nt-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">In recent years, it =

); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It’s an i=

=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=

x; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Put it this way. I can’t s=

6px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>I think that when tech pushes for less rest=

ickle-down economics</strong><span>: make us richer, and then we’ll m=

><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; ma=

rong><span>. </span></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size:=
 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Democrats and tech largely a=

e: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Democrats (broad brush) be=

 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">There’=

yle=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: =

lor: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px =
0;">Take NEPA. If you had said “NEPA” to someone in tech a deca=

hey might emit environmentally damaging amounts of vomit.  </p><p=
 style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margi=
n: 0 0 20px 0;">The National Environmental Policy Act is an example of a we=

p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; marg=
in: 0 0 20px 0;">If you want to build nuclear or solar, opponents can use N=

p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; m=
argin: 0 0 20px 0;">Klein describes the implementation of the CHIPS Act, a =

rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">I=
t’s no wonder, perhaps, that Elon Musk was one of the first major tec=
h CEOs to break for the Republicans, and that Founders Fund, which backed S=
paceX and has been investing in hard tech for many years, has long been the=
 venture firm that leans furthest to the right. They’ve been the ones=
 forced to deal with the most restrictive government regulations for the lo=
ngest.</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height:=
 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Mostly, it was relatively easy for tech compani=
es to avoid thinking about the burden of government regulations while they =
were focused purely on software. Now, the government has come for crypto an=
d AI, and startups have come for the physical world, and the Democrats and =
tech are on yet another inevitable collision course. </p><p style=3D"c=
olor: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px=
 0;">None of the regulation itself is new. The new piece is that more and m=
ore people in tech are funding and building companies that have to face the=

ocus America’s best and brightest away from “140 characters&#82=
21; and ad optimization towards renewable energy, defense, healthcare, tran=
sportation, education, and manufacturing. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(5=
4, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">They&#=
8217;re stepping back and saying, “Wait, are these just pretty words =

/p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; =

><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; ma=

p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; marg=
in: 0 0 20px 0;">Most people I know in tech got into it because there was s=
omething they really wanted to build, or because they saw it as the highest=
-leverage way to make a positive impact on millions of lives. The ones who =

 actually make that impact, it has to make economic sense and compete in th=
e free market. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px=
; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>As I was writing this, I sa=

x; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0; margin-left: 20px;"><em>With our =

ne-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">They want the chance to compete.&#160=
;</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px=
; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">In Casey’s case, if he succeeds, Terraform wil=

tyle=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin:=
 0 0 20px 0;">Thousands of other entrepreneurs are working on companies the=
y’ve chosen to dedicate their lives to because they believe they are =
civilizationally important, that they will ultimately matter more than whoe=
ver happens to be in the White House. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 5=
5, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Some will =
succeed wildly. Many of them will fail. They just want the chance to compet=
e. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-heigh=
t: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">They want to live in and contribute to a vigo=
rous nation that believes its best days are ahead. </p><p style=3D"col=
or: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0=
;">Despite what you’ve seen on Twitter or watched at the Republican N=
ational Convention, most of them don’t want to waste time pointing fi=
ngers or criticizing the other side for things that have gone wrong. David =
Sacks is not representative of tech. The vast majority of people in tech wo=
uld rather spend their time figuring out how to make things go right in the=
 future. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line=
-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Tech didn’t really want to get in=
volved in politics, but along the way, it got so big that a collision becam=
e inevitable. The Old Deal had to break. And faced with the choice of anoth=
er four years of the same animosity and roadblocks or something new, some c=
hose something new.</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px;=
 line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Now, all of a sudden, everything i=
s back on the table, somewhat less burdened by what has been, somewhat more=
 able to imagine what can be. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); =
font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">There’s a ch=
ance for the Democrats and tech to strike a New Deal. </p><h3 class=3D=

olor: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px=

 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;"><span>As my friend </span><a=

?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel st=

span> pointed out, we are already on an unsustainable path with too much de=
bt and increasing spending. “The way such trajectories normally end,&=
#8221; he said, “is in collapse or in some major world war that reset=

, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">But he also=
 points to the good news: we can innovate our way out of the problem. =
</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px;=
 margin: 0 0 20px 0;">We can have cheaper, more abundant energy. We can aut=

=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=

ng></p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26=
px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">While I don’t think it’s likely that D=

17;s necessary that they try. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); =
font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">So what would a Ne=

5, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It would c=

ht: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Both of those are relatively simple fixes. T=
rump has won tech’s support largely by paying lip service. It’s=

60;</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26=
px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Beyond that, though, it would be fantastic to see =
a party that leans into its new youthfulness to create excitement about the=
 future among its voters and makes an effort to explain that we’re al=
l better off when we’re working towards building something bigger tha=
n when we fight over scraps. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); f=
ont-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">If I’m tech, =
I’d even be willing to pay a higher tax rate on gains in exchange for=
 a smoother path towards creating them. If the promise is that we can make =
the country much richer, it shouldn’t hurt too bad. </p><p style=
=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0=
 20px 0;"><span>A New Deal would work with companies like </span><a href=3D=
"https://substack.com/redirect/3b1095c2-de3e-484f-a209-8da4eebbebfb?j=3DeyJ=
1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU" rel style=3D"c=

" rel style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); text-decoration: underline;">Cuby</a=
><span> that are working to give low-skilled workers good manufacturing and=
 construction jobs in order to increase employment and build up the pool of=
 talented Americans who know how to build. </span></p><p style=3D"colo=
r: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;=
">It would support automation efforts to ensure America’s manufacturi=
ng competitiveness without giving into the defeatist and wrong notion that =
robots will take our jobs.</p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size=
: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">It would enhance America&#8=
217;s capacity to install gigawatts of clean energy and economical climate =
technologies instead of limiting energy consumption. </p><p style=3D"color:=
 rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">=
It might even create regulatory sandboxes – both digital and in the p=
hysical world – where new technologies can be tested under looser con=
ditions until they’re ready for prime time. </p><p style=3D"colo=
r: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;=
">I’m not a policy wonk. Those are just a few off-the-dome suggestion=
s. What’s most important is lowering the hostility, starting the conv=
ersation, and remembering that American tech companies and the American gov=
ernment are on the same team. That we are a better nation when we build tog=
ether. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size: 16px; line-h=
eight: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">In an ideal world, the Democrats would st=
art to fix some of the things that ail the party, tech-specific or otherwis=
e. Specifically, put an end to Everything Bagel Liberalism at the Federal l=
evel so that it’s actually possible to implement the big, expensive b=
ills that they pass. </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55); font-size=
: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">If you need to piss off som=
e of the more extreme factions in the party, so be it… this is a part=
y that’s behind Donald Trump in the polls. If now isn’t a time =
for a hard reset, when will be? </p><p style=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55)=
; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 20px 0;">Maybe they can t=
